title: Design ART structure for a 50-person program with 3 value streams
prompt: We have a 50-person software program organized around 3 value streams: Customer Onboarding, Payments Processing, and Merchant Analytics. Each value stream has between 12 and 20 people. Design the ART structure — define team-of-teams composition, map teams to value streams, identify whether we need one ART or multiple, and flag any shared-service dependencies. Include a topology diagram and sizing validation.
expected_outputs: ART topology diagram mapping value streams to teams
Team roster with headcount and competency assignments
Sizing validation against SAFe 50-125 guideline
Cross-team dependency matrix
Recommendation on single ART vs. multiple ARTs with rationale

title: Plan PI Planning event for 8 teams with cross-team dependencies
prompt: We are preparing a PI Planning event for our ART which has 8 teams (5 feature teams, 2 platform teams, 1 UX/design team). There are known cross-team dependencies on a shared authentication service and a new API gateway. Plan the full PI Planning event: define the agenda, prepare the top features for breakout sessions, identify how to surface and manage the cross-team dependencies, set up the ROAM risk classification, and describe how the program board should be structured for 5 iterations plus an IP iteration.
expected_outputs: PI Planning agenda with timings for each session
Top features list prepared for team breakouts
Cross-team dependency identification and program board layout
ROAM risk register template with pre-identified risks
Confidence vote process and criteria

title: Assess SAFe implementation maturity and recommend improvements
prompt: Our ART just completed PI 4. Here are our results: 68% of committed PI objectives were met, average team velocity variance is 25%, cycle time increased 15% over the last 2 PIs, and three teams report recurring integration blockers. Conduct an Inspect & Adapt analysis — measure our PI performance quantitatively, perform root-cause analysis on the top impediments, prioritize improvement stories for PI 5, and produce a PI health scorecard with a maturity assessment and concrete recommendations.
expected_outputs: PI objective scorecard with achievement percentages
Flow metrics analysis with trend indicators
Root-cause analysis for top 3 impediments
Prioritized improvement backlog for PI 5 with WSJF scores
PI health scorecard with maturity assessment and recommendations

title: Connect ART execution to portfolio-level epic prioritization
prompt: Our Lean Portfolio Management team needs to decide on funding for 6 epics competing for next quarter's budget across 2 value streams. The total available budget is 120 FTE-months. Each epic has a lean business case draft but they have not been WSJF-ranked or validated against strategic themes. Connect our ART execution data (PI outcomes, velocity, flow metrics) to the portfolio decision — rank the epics using WSJF, validate alignment with our 3 strategic themes (Digital Expansion, Operational Efficiency, Customer Retention), enforce budget guardrails, and produce funding recommendations for the LPM review.
expected_outputs: Strategic theme traceability for all 6 epics
WSJF ranking table with cost-of-delay and job-size components
Budget allocation recommendation within 120 FTE-month guardrail
Portfolio Kanban snapshot showing epic pipeline state
Funding go/no-go recommendation per epic with rationale
